title: Fixer Artifact v1
description: Structured output produced by the fixer stage — the only stage in the pipeline permitted to modify files. It is a declaration of what the fixer did, checked by the host runner before anything is pushed: `input_sha` is compared against the frozen reviewed SHA, and the branch head is re-read to confirm it has not moved. The fixer's own claims are never trusted as authorization to push. See docs/ci-pipeline.md.

input_sha.description: The SHA the fixer believes it started from. The host verifies this equals the frozen reviewed SHA and aborts otherwise — a mismatch means the fixer worked from a tree nobody reviewed.

new_sha.description: Left null or set to the input SHA by the agent; the host overwrites it after committing. The agent never touches .git, so it cannot know this value.

mode.description: Whether the original authoring agent was resumed with its conversation intact, or a fresh agent read the reviewer artifacts cold. Recorded because the two have materially different authority: only a resumed author may push back on a reviewer by claiming its intent was misread.

summary.description: One paragraph, for the PR comment. Never quote runs/ content — this repository is public.

addressed.description: Blockers this fixer believes it resolved. Namespaced `<role>/<id>` matching the reviewer that raised them. The judge re-reviews from scratch on the next cycle; this list is a record, not a clearance.

id.description: Namespaced blocker id. Bare ids are rejected so two reviewers with colliding local ids cannot cross-clear each other.

resolution.description: `body_clarification` means no code changed — the fixer judged the reviewer misread the author's intent and sharpened the PR body instead. Only valid when mode is `author-resume`; a cold fixer has no intent to appeal to.

skipped.description: Blockers deliberately left alone. A skip is a normal outcome, not a failure: the safety gate is designed to skip anything it cannot fix mechanically and safely.

docs_updated.description: Any docs/*.md touched. This project is spec-driven: a code change that alters an invariant without a corresponding decision-log entry is drift, and the invariant reviewer will block it on the next cycle.

body_edited.description: Whether the fixer edited the PR body. Tracked because the body carries the `Resolves #N` and `Author-Session:` lines that author-resume depends on; an edit that drops them silently disables resume on the next cycle.
